content.method: Survey-based decomposition of admission score variance into tutoring-expenditure and prior-achievement components; natural experiment using historical admissions criteria changes (e.g., past desegregation rule shifts) to observe adaptive market response over time.
content.question: How much of the socioeconomic sorting into elite tracks is attributable to unequal access to private test preparation/tutoring versus differences in prior achievement, and would tutoring markets adapt to a redesigned admissions exam (T3) to reproduce the same SES advantage through new channels?
content.why_it_matters: T3's core mechanism assumes admissions redesign can reduce SES-linked sorting; if tutoring markets simply adapt, the reform would fail on its own terms despite requiring substantial institutional effort.
